items.title: TimerangeTV
items.text: With this MODX custom template variable two dependent timepicker inputs could be used to insert a time range in a MODX resource.

items.title: Requirements
items.text:
• MODX Revolution 2.6+
  • PHP 7.2+

items.title: Features
items.text: • Two dependent timepicker inputs allowing the backend user to insert a time range.

items.title: License
items.text: The project is licensed under the GPLv2 license.

items.title: Translations
items.text: Translations of the package can be made for the Default Lexicon and the System Setting Lexicon

items.title: Changelog
items.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog, and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Fix editing template variable properties in MODX 3.0.1

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Fix removing plugin events

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Fix OnDocFormRender was not found!

items.title: Changed
items.text:
• Code refactoring
  • Full MODX 3 compatibility

items.title: Fixed
items.text: • Fix missing values in the dropdown with a not default time increment setting

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Depend the timerange inputs on each other
  • Minimum/maximum values for the timerange values
  • TimerangeTV.Renderer usable i.e. as a Collections column renderer

items.title: Changed
items.text:
• PHP/MODX version check
  • Normalized/refactored code

items.title: Added
items.text: • Added German translation (Thanks to pepebe)

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Updated look of the time fields (Thanks to pepebe)

items.title: Changed
items.text: • Setting values back to the fields when loading resource edit form

items.title: Added
items.text:
• Initial project
  • Initial changelog started

items.title: Contributors
items.text:
The TimerangeTV project was started in 2012 by Bert Oost and is maintained and developed further since 2019 by Thomas Jakobi.

Many thanks to all who contributed, whether by creating pull requests, submitting bug reports, or donating.

items.title: Install from MODX Extras
items.text: Search for TimerangeTV in the Package Manager of your MODX installation and install it in there.

items.title: Manual installation
items.text:
If you can't access the MODX Extras Repository in your MODX installation, you can manually install TimerangeTV.

  • Download the transport package from MODX Extras (or one of the pre built transport packages in _packages)
  • Upload the zip file to your MODX installation's core/packages folder or upload it manually in the MODX Package Manager.
  • In the MODX Manager, navigate to the Package Manager page, and select 'Search locally for packages' from the dropdown button.
  • TimerangeTV should now show up in the list of available packages. Click the corresponding 'Install' button and follow the instructions to complete the installation.

items.title: Build it from source
items.text: To build and install the package from source you could use Git Package Management. The GitHub repository of TimerangeTV contains a config.json to build that package locally. Use this option, if you want to debug TimerangeTV and/or contribute bugfixes and enhancements.

items.title: Input Options
items.text:
The following input options could be set in template variable settings:

Setting Description Default Allow Blank If set to No, MODX will not allow the user to save the Resource until a valid, non-blank value has been entered in the From Date input. Yes Maximum Value The maximum value of the time inputs. 23:59 Minimum Value The minimum value of the time inputs. 00:00 Time Format The format must be valid according to Ext JS Date System setting manager_time_format Time Increment The incremental number of minutes for the time lists 60
